enterprise_management.mechanism: Organizations can provide default MCP server configurations via a .well-known/opencode endpoint; users can override remote defaults in their local opencode.json; local config values take precedence over remote defaults

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Remote MCP server headers and OAuth fields support {env:VAR_NAME} syntax for environment variable interpolation; local servers accept an environment object with key-value pairs

oauth_support.mechanism: Remote MCP servers support OAuth 2.0 authentication with Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591). OpenCode auto-detects 401 responses and initiates the OAuth flow, storing tokens in ~/.local/share/opencode/mcp-auth.json. Pre-registered client credentials can be supplied via the oauth object (clientId, clientSecret, scope). OAuth can be disabled per-server by setting oauth: false.

tool_filtering.mechanism: MCP tools are filtered via the global tools map in opencode.json using the server name as a prefix (e.g., mymcpservername_*: false); glob patterns (* and ?) are supported; per-agent tool access is controlled via the agent's permission or tools map

transport_types.mechanism: OpenCode supports two transport types: local (type: local, launched via command array with stdio communication) and remote (type: remote, connected via URL supporting HTTP/SSE streamable-HTTP)

providerExtensions.name: Local (stdio) MCP transport
providerExtensions.summary: Local MCP servers are launched via a command array (command field) with optional environment variables (environment field). Communicate over stdio. Type must be set to local.

providerExtensions.name: Remote (HTTP/SSE) MCP transport
providerExtensions.summary: Remote MCP servers are connected via URL (url field) with optional headers object. Type must be set to remote. Supports OAuth 2.0 and custom authorization headers.

providerExtensions.name: Server enabled/disabled flag
providerExtensions.summary: Each MCP server entry supports an enabled boolean flag to temporarily disable a server without removing its configuration. Defaults to enabled.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server tool fetch timeout
providerExtensions.summary: The timeout field (in milliseconds, default 5000) controls how long OpenCode waits for the MCP server to respond with its tool list on startup.

providerExtensions.name: JSONC config file format
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers are configured under the mcp key in opencode.json or opencode.jsonc at the project root or ~/.config/opencode/. JSONC allows comments.

providerExtensions.name: OAuth 2.0 configuration object
providerExtensions.summary: Remote servers support an oauth configuration object with clientId, clientSecret, and scope fields for pre-registered client credentials. Setting oauth: false disables automatic OAuth detection for servers using API keys.

providerExtensions.name: Organization remote MCP defaults
providerExtensions.summary: Organizations can publish default MCP server configurations via a .well-known/opencode HTTP endpoint. Users can opt-in to specific servers by enabling them in their local config; local config overrides remote defaults.

providerExtensions.name: MCP auth management CLI commands
providerExtensions.summary: opencode mcp auth <server-name> triggers OAuth browser flow; opencode mcp list shows all servers and auth status; opencode mcp logout <server-name> removes stored credentials; opencode mcp debug <server-name> diagnoses auth and connectivity.
